content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be adapted to create a distributed shared curiosity memory layer for A2A protocol networks, where agents collectively curate high-information unlabeled interaction data by offloading their curiosity-driven exploration traces to a shared active learning memory pool. This shared pool lets individual agents avoid redundant low-value exploration of unknown interaction spaces, while the A2A protocol enables synchronized query selection to prioritize the most uncertain cross-agent interaction patterns for collective fine-tuning.
